title: Matricaria monticola
taxonomy: Matricaria monticola is classified in the family Asteraceae. The plant is also known as mountain chamomile or Montane mayweed. It belongs to the genus Matricaria, which includes several species of flowering plants. Matricaria monticola is native to Europe and Asia, and it can be found in mountainous regions. Its classification further categorizes it as a species of perennial herb.
uses: Matricaria monticola is a plant species that has been found to possess medicinal properties, although research is limited. It has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to inflammatory conditions. The plant is thought to contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its potential health benefits. Some studies have suggested that Matricaria monticola may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential therapeutic applications.
benefits: Matricaria monticola is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds may help alleviate stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation and better sleep. These compounds have been identified as contributing to the plant's anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The sesquiterpene lactones, specifically, have been isolated and studied for their potential therapeutic applications. The flavonoids present in the plant, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents and their effects on human health.

intro: Matricaria monticola, also known as Mountain chamomile, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, closely related to the common chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in soothing digestive issues, calming the nervous system, and promoting sleep. The bioactive constituents of Matricaria monticola include flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpene lactones, such as matricine, apigenin, and chamazulene, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Matricaria monticola is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and gastrointestinal disorders.
